vitals.name: Sierra Nevada Límite Gran Fondo
vitals.location: Cenes de la Vega, Granada, Spain

adventure.explanation: The 146km route through Granada's UNESCO Biosphere Reserve finishes at Pradollano in Sierra Nevada at 2100m, where participants in 2018 crossed the line amid snowflakes.[4][5]

altitude.explanation: Reaching a high point of 2100m at the Sierra Nevada ski resort finish line, this event delivers extreme high-altitude exposure throughout its demanding parcours.[1][5]

climate.explanation: May 23 races in Sierra Nevada face variable weather with cold temperatures at 2100m altitudes, potentially including snow even in late spring.[1][4]

community.explanation: Bib collection occurs at Polideportivo Municipal de Cenes de la Vega on May 22 from 11:00-14:00 and 17:00-20:30, plus Saturday 06:00-08:00, fostering pre-race gatherings.[1][2]

elevation.explanation: The Gran Fondo features ~3900m of cumulative elevation gain across 146km, tackling ports like Blancares in Quéntar and La Sabina.[1][4]

expenses.explanation: Entry fee for the SNL Gran Fondo stands at 55€, with a combo ticket for Gran Fondo plus Subida al Veleta at 80€, providing access to services like bag drop and nutrition stations.[8][2]

experience.explanation: Cyclists enjoy a circular 146km route starting at 8:00am from Pradollano, complete with finisher medals, timing chips, drink stations, and bike washing at the Sierra Nevada finish.[1][2]

field_depth.explanation: No participant numbers or past results are detailed for the May 23, 2026 event, limiting evidence of competitive depth.[1]

length.explanation: The flagship Gran Fondo covers 146km in a circular loop from Pradollano through Granada province, passing El Duque and Fuente de la Gitana.[1][4]

logistics.explanation: Organizers provide bib pickup at Cenes de la Vega sports pavilion, guardrop service, mechanical assistance, and a 9-hour cutoff for the 146km route starting at 8:00am.[2][3]

race_quality.explanation: Route includes multiple aid stations with nutrition and drinks, medical assistance, and medals categorized by time, gender, and age for the 146km distance.[2][4]

technicality.explanation: Demands technical skill on climbs to Port of Blancares in La Peza and descents through Güejar Sierra back to Pradollano at 2100m.[1][3]

value.explanation: For 55€, entrants receive timing chip, finisher medal, goodie bag, nutrition stations, bag drop, and showers at the 2100m Sierra Nevada finish.[2][8]

cultural_impact.explanation: Deepens Granada's cycling heritage in Andalusia.

prestige.explanation: Lacking UCI endorsement or mentions in major calendars despite its 3900m climbs to 2100m in Sierra Nevada, it holds emblematic status only nationally.[1][9]

course_description.character: Brutal high-altitude road gran fondo through Sierra Nevada's demanding alpine passes, blending endurance suffering with panoramic mountain beauty.

suffering_zones.label: Initial Ascent from Cenes de la Vega
suffering_zones.description: Steep ramps out of the lowlands quickly sap early energy before the main Sierra Nevada walls.

suffering_zones.label: Mid-Route Mountain Passes
suffering_zones.description: Relentless climbs through Genil Pines and Las Sabinas Pass accumulate massive vertical.

suffering_zones.label: Final Push to Pradollano Finish
suffering_zones.description: Highest efforts near 2100-3100m altitude where thin air and fatigue collide.

course_description.signature_challenge: The continuous 38km+ mountain passes culminating near Veleta peak at over 3100m, Europe's highest road finish line.

logistics.overview: Efficiently organized with staggered starts (146km at 8AM, 114km at 9:30AM), bib pickup at Polideportivo Municipal de Cenes de la Vega, bag drop, bike wash, and finish services at Pradollano.
logistics.accommodation: Collaborations with TODOSIERRANEVADA for discounts; Granada city offers charming hotels minutes away.
logistics.getting_there: Fly into Granada Airport (GRX), short drive to Cenes de la Vega; accessible from Malaga too.
logistics.parking: Available at start in Cenes de la Vega sports complex.
logistics.spectator_info: Prime viewing at start/finish in Cenes de la Vega and Pradollano summit area; follow via live tracking.

climate.overview: Classic Sierra Nevada spring: warm valley starts (60-70°F) contrast cold high-altitude summits (30-50°F), potential snow even in May.
climate.precipitation_chance: Moderate, with possible flurries at elevation.
climate.wind_conditions: Gusty crosswinds on exposed passes amplify difficulty.
climate.altitude_effects: Significant above 2500m: hypoxia, cold, and rapid weather shifts demand acclimation.

terrain.terrain_overview: Primarily paved roads through Granada province featuring extreme vertical climbs from lowlands to alpine zones.
terrain.key_features: Iconic Sierra Nevada ascents including A-395 highway, Dornajo, Las Sabinas Pass, Hoya de la Mora; max altitude near Veleta peak.

history.overview: Emblematic Spanish gran fondo showcasing Granada's cycling heritage with Gran Fondo and Subida al Veleta formats.
history.notable_editions: 2018 edition saw snow at 2100m finish; 2025 introduced more circular two-day epic route.
history.growth_trajectory: Steadily growing as must-do national challenge with international buzz via new routes and organization.

final_verdict.one_liner: Ultimate Spanish climbing crucible for masochists who crave thin air and endless VAM.
final_verdict.best_for: Climbing specialists, altitude junkies, and endurance roadies seeking iconic Euro suffering.
final_verdict.not_ideal_for: Beginners, sprinters, or anyone fearing high-altitude cold and 11,000ft of pain.
final_verdict.pro_tip: Acclimatize in Granada 2-3 days prior; layer for 40°F summit temps; fuel heavily before the big passes.

citations.title: Sierra Nevada Límite Gran Fondo, 23 May 2026 | World's Marathons
citations.snippet: SIERRA NEVADA LÍMITE GRAN FONDO is a cycling experience that connects sport and nature, cycling through the most spectacular and demanding landscapes.

citations.title: Sierra Nevada Límite
citations.snippet: More than just a race, SIERRA NEVADA LÍMITE is a cycling experience that combines sport and nature.

citations.title: Sierra Nevada Limite - Gran Fondo 2026 - Finishers
citations.snippet: 146 km (3,900 mD+) and 114 km (3,050 mD+), finishing at 2,100m above sea level.

citations.title: Sierra Nevada Limite Subida al Veleta 2026
citations.snippet: Highest finish line in Europe at 3100m, 38km with 2250m elevation gain.

citations.snippet: Saturday, May 23, 2026 at Sierra Nevada, Granada, Spain.
